title: Similarity-weighted undercut pressure features
summary: Public F1 strategy literature stresses that ignoring competitors materially changes optimal pit timing; the game-theory paper reports large odds losses when competition is ignored.
rationale: Public F1 strategy literature stresses that ignoring competitors materially changes optimal pit timing; the game-theory paper reports large odds losses when competition is ignored. Your current field features are broad averages and simple pit-wave counts, but undercut risk is local: nearby rivals on fresher tyres or who just pitted matter more than the whole field.
implementation_hint: Within each Year/Race/Lap, construct local opponent features for cars in nearby positions or with similar lap pace/tyre age: count of nearby rivals who pitted in last 1-2 laps, weighted average of rivals' tyre-life deficit, fraction of nearby cars on fresher compound, and a soft kNN score over position plus lap-delta plus tyre-life similarity. Add interactions with current position and positive lap-time loss.
expected_effect: Can capture tactical stop triggers that broad race aggregates miss, improving recall on competitive pit calls around undercut/overcut windows.
risk: No true interval gaps are available, so proximity is only approximated by position and pace; noisy local features may overfit if not smoothed.
